title: Connected and Multi Agent Patterns
summary: Connected or multi-agent patterns let agents delegate to other agents or expose curated capabilities through agent-to-agent links, MCP endpoints, or Foundry Toolboxes.
analogy: It is a command structure: the operations officer coordinates, the medic handles medical tasks, the logistics officer handles supplies, and each has only the equipment needed for that role.
diagram:
graph TD
Orchestrator[Coordinator agent] --> Research[Research agent]
Orchestrator --> Action[Action agent]
Research --> Toolbox[Shared MCP toolbox]
Action --> Service[Backend service]
Toolbox --> Server[MCP servers]
body:
## First principles

First principles: complex work is often a team sport. Instead of one giant agent with every permission, split roles so each agent has a small mission, small toolset, and small identity blast radius.

## Definition

Connected or multi-agent patterns let agents delegate to other agents or expose curated capabilities through agent-to-agent links, MCP endpoints, or Foundry Toolboxes. This lane keeps it brief: MCP can act as the shared tool boundary, while dedicated agents own specialist reasoning.

## How it fits together

Prefer narrow specialist agents over one all-powerful agent when permissions, audit trails, or domain instructions differ. Use MCP or Toolbox to share common tools without copying configuration everywhere.

## Worked example

```python
# This runnable planning helper prevents accidental all powerful agent designs.
agents = {
    "coordinator": {"tools": [], "permission": "no direct writes"},
    "researcher": {"tools": ["search_docs", "read_doc"], "permission": "read only"},
    "operator": {"tools": ["create_ticket"], "permission": "write approved tickets only"},
}

for name, policy in agents.items():
    print(f"{name}: tools={policy['tools']} permission={policy['permission']}")
```

*Represent a simple routing table before implementing multi-agent calls*

drills.a: To separate instructions, permissions, blast radius, and audit trails.

drills.q: What can MCP provide in a multi-agent design?
drills.a: A shared standard endpoint for tools used by multiple agents or frameworks.

drills.q: What is the danger of one mega-agent?
drills.a: It tends to accumulate too many tools, roles, and permissions.

teachItBack: Multi-agent design is permission design. Split roles when authority differs. Use MCP or Toolbox as a shared tool plane, not as an excuse to give every agent every capability.

glossary.definition: Agent or code that routes work among specialists.
Preview Foundry bundle of tools exposed as an MCP-compatible endpoint.

sources.title: Microsoft Learn: What is Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
